notes: Treat the White Dwarf 125 reference to White Dwarf 123 as a publication-delay typo; the first campaign part used here is White Dwarf 124 pages 18-31.
This file defines sequence structure only. Damaged numeric tables remain pending proofread in docs/rules/clean/campaign.md.

terms.contractSequence: A campaign time block containing attack declarations, engagements, and post-engagement consequences.
terms.unit: A persistent Op, agency, outlaw gang, or renegade Op controlled by a player.
terms.engagement: A tactical roadfight scheduled by an attack declaration.
terms.activeVehicle: A vehicle with working engine, usable non-passive ammunition and firing means, and a non-disabled driver.

intercept.setupSummary: Approach winner chooses which road end they start from. Approach loser places vehicles at the opposite end, anywhere within the first two track sections, facing the other end and under cruising restrictions. Approach winner then places vehicles within the first two sections at the nominated end, heading toward the middle and without cruising restrictions.

pursuit.setupSummary: Approach loser places vehicles on the fourth track section facing toward the seventh section and under cruising restrictions. Approach winner starts on the first track section facing along the rest of the track, at minimum 60 mph unless on a curve where safe speed is the minimum, and no faster than vehicle top speed.

ambush.setupSummary: Approach loser places vehicles on the middle track section, chooses one direction of travel for all loser vehicles, and is under cruising restrictions. Approach winner places vehicles on the two sections at either or both road ends, facing any direction unless behind the target, where they must face the target. The ambusher may also place vehicles in the two edge lanes of any section; these start at 20 mph/speed factor 1 and may face either way.

engagementEndAndSalvage.endCondition: Engagement continues until only one side has active vehicles in play.

preEngagementSteps.summary: Players roll for declaration order, then eligible units declare attacks subject to one-attack and one-target restrictions.
rules: Each unit may make only one attack declaration per contract sequence.
Each unit may be the target of only one attack declaration per contract sequence.
A unit already targeted by an attack declaration cannot declare its own attack.
The two units must form a legal engagement pairing.
A unit that declines an attack declaration retires immediately and cannot be replaced until the next contract sequence.

preEngagementSteps.summary: For each declared engagement, allocate participating drivers and vehicles, buy any information bonuses, roll approach, and choose intercept, pursuit, or ambush as allowed.
Initialize attacker objective tracking; the attacking unit must spend six phases with at least one attacker within four spaces of at least one defender unless exempt.
rules: Objective is tracked for the unit that is attacker for the engagement, not necessarily the approach winner.
Different vehicle pairs may satisfy different phases.
Ambushed units are exempt from objective failure penalties.
Unavoidable inactive/lost attacking vehicles, crashes or loss of control before contact, and defender escape/removal before objective range are objective failure exceptions.
Deliberate failure causes pay/mileage/loot penalties and can remove the unit after repeated failure.

preEngagementSteps.summary: Play the tactical roadfight using core, White Line Fever, and applicable campaign setup rules.

postEngagementPhases.summary: Resolve driver death, injury, limb disabled results, recovery clauses, and escape/rescue.
Determine salvage rights and pay immediate scavenger costs to recover eligible equipment or vehicles.
Surviving drivers gain mileage from combat or flight engagement tables; drive skill updates immediately at thresholds.
Ops claim bounty for qualifying outlaw losses; outlaws and renegade Ops resolve loot.
Apply engagement and pending media psychosis, test drivers with sufficient psychosis, and resolve disorders.
Recalculate kudos and media visibility, optionally resolve media rolls, then check media manipulation.
Form agencies, replace losses, hire novice or experienced drivers, and apply agency/gang obligations.
Repair, reload, sell surplus, redesign vehicles, buy new vehicles/equipment, fit cybernetics/software, and repair hack damage.
Persist updated driver, vehicle, unit, funds, store, obligation, and campaign state before the next contract sequence.

campaignEndConditions.summary: If only one unit remains in the campaign, that unit's controlling player wins.
If more than one unit remains but all are Op units, the campaign ends as a side victory for law and order unless one or more units become renegade.
If no opposing units are available for a contract sequence, the campaign ends.
